tldr: Headline framing splits into four tracks: outbreak containment and quarantine (dominant, 16 buckets), government border-screening policies (4 buckets), COVID-era panic and misinformation recycling (2 buckets), and quarantine as lived experience (2 buckets). Indonesia is the most isolated at 0.350, covering the story solely through local airport supervision; The Intercept uniquely frames it as a COVID-misinformation revival story.
event_summary: Eight passengers from the MV Hondius cruise ship tested positive for Andes hantavirus; three have died and six arrived in Australia for quarantine. The WHO confirmed the outbreak.

evidence.quote: World Health Organization confirms eight cases of Andes hantavirus
Rare virus outbreak kills travelers on Atlantic cruise ship
Banten tightens supervision at Soetta Airport to prevent hantavirus
Vietnam, Cambodia strengthen airport screening for hantavirus
Amid Hantavirus Panic, the Ivermectin Super Fans Are Back
Warum das Hantavirus nicht mit Corona vergleichbar ist
A Scientist's Close Call with Hantavirus Aboard the M.V. Hondius
Temperatura medida duas vezes ao dia e pedidos de Starbucks

isolation_top.note: Covers story entirely through local airport border-security lens — Banten province and Soekarno-Hatta Airport supervision — diverging from the outbreak/quarantine framing dominant across all other buckets.
DW headline explicitly compares hantavirus to COVID and invokes epidemic classification terminology — both a language artefact and a distinctive conceptual angle not present elsewhere.
The Intercept headline frames the outbreak as COVID-misinformation recycling (ivermectin super fans), diverging entirely from the medical/quarantine coverage dominant elsewhere.
New Yorker runs a first-person scientist-aboard headline — diverges from third-person outbreak coverage dominant across the corpus.
O Globo's quarantine-diarist headline (temperature checks and Starbucks orders) is entirely personal-narrative in a corpus dominated by epidemiological coverage.

silences.note: Nordic headlines treat hantavirus as a sidebar item within generic health roundups — no bucket-defining frame emerges from the headline alone.

single_outlet_findings.finding: The only US outlet in the corpus; its headline frames the outbreak as a COVID-misinformation recurrence story (ivermectin super fans), making no reference to quarantine logistics or case counts that dominate all other buckets.
Quarantine-diarist headline (temperature checks twice daily, Starbucks orders) — the only first-person experiential frame in a corpus otherwise dominated by epidemiological third-person reporting.
Scientist-aboard framing ('A Scientist's Close Call') signals eyewitness personal narrative — unique in the corpus and contributes to opinion_magazines being the fourth most isolated bucket.

bottom_line: Headline framing is dominated by the HEALTH_SAFETY outbreak-and-quarantine track (16 of 25 buckets). The two most distinctive headline signals are Indonesia's pure border-security framing (no outbreak content) and The Intercept's COVID-misinformation-recycling frame. Germany and the USA jointly constitute the PUBLIC_OPINION cluster through the COVID comparison lens — Germany from a debunking angle, the USA from a misinformation-critique angle.
